title: Contributing to baselinr
text: Open issue first discuss change. Fork repository create branch. Keep style consistent (package uses styler lintr); add testthat tests new behavior; run devtools::check() make sure passes cleanly. Open pull request describing changed .

title: Contributing to baselinr
text: Please respectful constructive. participating agree keep interactions welcoming everyone.

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: Every quasi-experimental impact study education answer question anyone looks outcomes: treatment comparison groups similar enough baseline? Works Clearinghouse (WWC) sets de facto standard education research: covariate standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) 0.05 less satisfies baseline equivalence ; 0.05 0.25, equivalence holds covariate statistically adjusted impact model; 0.25, covariate establish equivalence. baselinr computes effect sizes categories baseline table something assemble hand every report.

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: default, every numeric, logical, factor column treatment indicator treated covariate. covariate exactly two unique values treated binary summarized Cox index; numeric covariates use Hedges’ g. Pass covariates = control set explicitly.
code: study <- data.frame(   treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6), # continuous -> Hedges' g   female = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1) # binary     -> Cox index )  baseline_equivalence(study, treatment = "treat") #>   covariate       type n_treatment n_comparison mean_treatment mean_comparison #> 1   pretest continuous           3            3      6.0000000       5.0000000 #> 2    female     binary           3            3      0.6666667       0.3333333 #>   sd_treatment sd_comparison effect_size  wwc_category #> 1    1.0000000     1.0000000   0.8000000 not_satisfied #> 2    0.5773503     0.5773503   0.8401784 not_satisfied

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: baseline_equivalence() built exported helpers can also call directly.
code: # Standardized mean difference (Hedges' g) for a continuous covariate hedges_g(study$pretest, study$treat) #> [1] 0.8  # Cox index for a binary covariate cox_index(study$female, study$treat) #> [1] 0.8401784  # Classify any effect size(s) into the WWC categories wwc_classify(c(0.03, 0.12, 0.80)) #> [1] "satisfied"                 "satisfied_with_adjustment" #> [3] "not_satisfied"

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: Love plot shows standardized effect size covariate WWC thresholds (0.05 0.25), coloured category:  report-ready table, gt_baseline() returns formatted gt table:

title: Getting started with baselinr
text: Continuous covariates use Hedges’ g (WWC small-sample correction); binary covariates use WWC Cox index. Collapse table overall verdict wwc_summary(), assess sample loss attrition(), visualise love_plot(), format gt_baseline(). See NEWS.md roadmap.

what: Step 1: assess baseline equivalence, before looking at the outcome
title: An impact-evaluation workflow
text: credibility later effect estimate rests whether two groups comparable baseline. pass baseline covariates explicitly. include posttest, outcome, baseline covariate. baselinr automatically uses Hedges’ g continuous covariates (pretest, attendance, age) Cox index binary ones (female, frpl, ell).
code: baseline_covs <- c("pretest", "attendance", "age", "female", "frpl", "ell")  equiv <- baseline_equivalence(tutoring, treatment = "treat",                               covariates = baseline_covs)  knitr::kable(equiv, digits = 3)

title: An impact-evaluation workflow
text: covariate falls one three Works Clearinghouse categories: satisfied: groups equivalent covariate; nothing . satisfied_with_adjustment: equivalence holds statistically adjust covariate impact model. commitment, pass: covariates must appear model. not_satisfied: covariate establish equivalence even adjustment. ’s threat study’s credibility confront, bury.
code: equiv[, c("covariate", "effect_size", "wwc_category")] #>    covariate effect_size              wwc_category #> 1    pretest  0.19639834 satisfied_with_adjustment #> 2 attendance  0.18641935 satisfied_with_adjustment #> 3        age  0.01971405                 satisfied #> 4     female  0.01215809                 satisfied #> 5       frpl  0.26775010             not_satisfied #> 6        ell  0.38544774             not_satisfied

title: An impact-evaluation workflow
text: dashed lines mark 0.05 0.25 thresholds; points coloured category. plot makes -risk covariates obvious glance.

what: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: baselinr builds report-ready baseline equivalence tables impact evaluations education research, following conventions Works Clearinghouse (WWC). Given treatment indicator set covariates, reports appropriate standardized effect size covariate, Hedges’ g continuous covariates Cox index binary ones, together WWC equivalence category. thin, education-specific reporting layer. general-purpose covariate balance assessment, see cobalt; baselinr focuses narrowly WWC equivalence categories education evaluation reports required state.

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub:

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: WWC categories :
code: library(baselinr)  study <- data.frame(   treat   = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6), # continuous -> Hedges' g   female  = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1)  # binary     -> Cox index )  knitr::kable(baseline_equivalence(study, treatment = "treat"), digits = 3)

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: love_plot() shows standardized effect size every covariate WWC thresholds (requires ggplot2):  gt_baseline() renders table formatted gt table reports Quarto/HTML (requires gt):

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: Continuous covariates use Hedges’ g (WWC small-sample correction); binary covariates (numeric 0/1, logical, two-level factor) use WWC Cox index. wwc_summary() collapses table overall verdict, attrition() reports overall differential attrition. See NEWS.md roadmap.

title: WWC-Aligned Baseline Equivalence Tables for Education Impact Evaluations
text: MIT © Yuxia Liang

title: Summary
text: quasi-experimental randomized impact evaluations education programs, analysts must demonstrate treatment comparison groups similar baseline claim program effects credible. U.S. education research, Works Clearinghouse (WWC) sets de facto standard judgment: baseline covariate summarized standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) classified one three categories determine whether covariate must handled impact model [@wwc2022]. baselinr small R package automates specific, repetitive task. Given data frame, treatment indicator, set covariates, returns report-ready table containing group’s sample size summary statistics; appropriate standardized effect size — Hedges’ g (WWC small-sample correction) continuous covariates Cox index binary covariates; corresponding WWC baseline-equivalence category.

title: Summary
text: Assessing baseline equivalence WWC conventions routine error-prone hand-work: analysts repeatedly compute Hedges’ g small-sample correction factor, apply 0.05 0.25 thresholds, assemble table every report, often spreadsheet software ad hoc scripts. General-purpose R tools covariate balance, cobalt [@greifer2023], compute wide range balance statistics matching weighting workflows encode specific WWC reporting categories education evaluation reports required state. baselinr deliberately occupies narrow gap tools: thin, education-specific reporting layer produces exact baseline-equivalence categorization expected WWC-aligned reporting, modeling assumptions minimal interface. intended users applied education researchers, evaluators, graduate students need reproducible, transparent baseline table rather general causal-inference framework.

title: Summary
text: hedges_g() computes standardized mean difference treatment comparison group continuous covariate, using pooled within-group standard deviation small-sample correction factor ω=1−3/(4N−9)\omega = 1 - 3 / (4N - 9), NN combined sample size [@hedges1981]. cox_index() computes WWC Cox index binary covariate, dCox=(logit(pt)−logit(pc))/1.65d_{Cox} = (\mathrm{logit}(p_t) - \mathrm{logit}(p_c)) / 1.65, placing difference proportions scale comparable Hedges’ g [@wwc2022]. wwc_classify() maps standardized effect sizes three WWC categories: satisfied (|es|≤0.05|es| \le 0.05), satisfied_with_adjustment (0.05<|es|≤0.250.05 < |es| \le 0.25), not_satisfied (|es|>0.25|es| > 0.25) [@wwc2022]. baseline_equivalence() combines one-row-per-covariate table group sizes, summary statistics, appropriate effect size, WWC category, choosing Hedges’ g Cox index based whether covariate continuous binary. love_plot() gt_baseline() present table Love plot standardized effect sizes (WWC threshold lines) formatted gt table, respectively. wwc_summary() collapses table overall equivalence verdict, attrition() reports overall differential attrition — inputs WWC attrition standard — package covers pillars WWC group-design quality. package depends base R [@rcoreteam], covered unit tests, ships worked example vignette.

title: Summary
code: library(baselinr)  study <- data.frame(   treat   = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6), # continuous -> Hedges' g   female  = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1)  # binary     -> Cox index )  baseline_equivalence(study, treatment = "treat") #>   covariate       type n_treatment n_comparison mean_treatment mean_comparison #> 1   pretest continuous           3            3      6.0000000       5.0000000 #> 2    female     binary           3            3      0.6666667       0.3333333 #>   sd_treatment sd_comparison effect_size  wwc_category #> 1    1.0000000     1.0000000   0.8000000 not_satisfied #> 2    0.5773503     0.5773503   0.8401784 not_satisfied

title: Summary
text: baselinr handles continuous covariates (Hedges’ g) binary covariates (Cox index), presents results Love plot (love_plot()) formatted gt table (gt_baseline()). baselinr reports baseline-equivalence statistics; select covariates, fit impact models, make adequacy determinations user’s behalf.

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
text: Computes overall differential sample attrition two-group design, inputs Works Clearinghouse (WWC) attrition standard. Differential attrition absolute difference treatment comparison attrition rates.

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
text: treatment Vector identifying group membership; exactly two unique non-missing values (larger treated treatment group, hedges_g()). retained Logical (0/1) length treatment: TRUE/1 cases retained analytic sample, FALSE/0 lost. na.rm Logical; drop rows treatment retained NA. Default TRUE.

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
text: one-row data frame columns attrition_overall, attrition_treatment, attrition_comparison, differential_attrition (proportions).

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
text: function reports attrition rates; classify . Compare overall differential rates WWC attrition boundary chosen response assumption (cautious optimistic) Procedures Handbook.

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Overall and differential attrition — attrition
code: set.seed(1) g <- rep(c(1, 0), each = 100) kept <- rbinom(200, 1, ifelse(g == 1, 0.9, 0.8)) attrition(g, kept) #>   attrition_overall attrition_treatment attrition_comparison #> 1              0.12                0.06                 0.18 #>   differential_attrition #> 1                   0.12

what: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: Builds report-ready baseline-equivalence table set covariates, reporting group sample sizes, summaries, appropriate standardized effect size, corresponding Works Clearinghouse (WWC) equivalence category covariate. Continuous covariates use Hedges' g; binary covariates use Cox index.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: data data frame. treatment String naming column data identifies group membership. Must exactly two unique non-missing values (see hedges_g() treatment group determined). covariates Character vector column names evaluate. Defaults numeric, logical, factor columns data treatment.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: data frame one row per covariate columns: covariate; type ("continuous" "binary"); n_treatment, n_comparison; mean_treatment, mean_comparison (group means continuous covariates, event proportions binary ones); sd_treatment, sd_comparison; effect_size (Hedges' g Cox index, per type); wwc_category.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: covariate exactly two unique non-missing values treated binary; numeric covariate treated continuous. non-numeric covariate two categories supported raises error.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Baseline equivalence table for an impact evaluation — baseline_equivalence
code: df <- data.frame(   treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6),   female = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1) ) baseline_equivalence(df, treatment = "treat") #>   covariate       type n_treatment n_comparison mean_treatment mean_comparison #> 1   pretest continuous           3            3      6.0000000       5.0000000 #> 2    female     binary           3            3      0.6666667       0.3333333 #>   sd_treatment sd_comparison effect_size  wwc_category #> 1    1.0000000     1.0000000   0.8000000 not_satisfied #> 2    0.5773503     0.5773503   0.8401784 not_satisfied

what: baselinr: WWC-aligned baseline equivalence for education impact evaluations — baselinr-package
title: baselinr: WWC-aligned baseline equivalence for education impact evaluations — baselinr-package
text: baselinr produces report-ready baseline equivalence tables impact evaluations education research, following conventions Works Clearinghouse (WWC). takes data frame, treatment indicator, set covariates, reports, covariate, appropriate standardized effect size (Hedges' g continuous covariates, Cox index binary covariates) together WWC baseline-equivalence category.

title: baselinr: WWC-aligned baseline equivalence for education impact evaluations — baselinr-package
text: Maintainer: Yuxia Liang zl1212@uw.edu (ORCID) Authors: Yuxia Liang zl1212@uw.edu (ORCID)

what: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
text: Computes Works Clearinghouse (WWC) Cox index, standardized effect size binary (dichotomous) covariate. Cox index places difference two proportions scale comparable Hedges' g, can classified baseline-equivalence thresholds.

title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
text: x binary covariate (numeric 0/1, logical, two-level factor, vector exactly two unique non-missing values). larger value (e.g. 1, TRUE, second sorted level) treated "event". treatment Vector length x identifying group membership; exactly two unique non-missing values (see hedges_g()). na.rm Logical; drop rows x treatment NA. Default TRUE.

title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
text: single numeric value: Cox index. Returns NA (warning) group proportion exactly 0 1, index undefined.

title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
text: index \(d_{Cox} = (\mathrm{logit}(p_t) - \mathrm{logit}(p_c)) / 1.65\), \(p_t\) \(p_c\) proportions "event" category treatment comparison groups.

title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Cox index for a binary covariate — cox_index
code: x <- c(1, 1, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0) g <- c(1, 1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0) cox_index(x, g) #> Warning: Cox index is undefined when a group proportion is 0 or 1; returning NA. #> [1] NA

what: Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
title: Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
text: Renders result baseline_equivalence() formatted gt table rounded statistics readable column labels. Requires gt package.

title: Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
text: equivalence data frame returned baseline_equivalence(). decimals Number decimal places numeric columns. Default 2.

title: Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
text: gt_tbl object.

title: Format a baseline equivalence table with gt — gt_baseline
code: if (requireNamespace("gt", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(     treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),     pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6),     female = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1)   )   tbl <- gt_baseline(baseline_equivalence(df, "treat")) }

what: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: Computes standardized mean difference (Hedges' g) treatment comparison group single numeric covariate, using pooled within-group standard deviation small-sample correction factor used Works Clearinghouse (WWC).

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: x Numeric vector covariate values. treatment Vector length x identifying group membership. Must exactly two unique non-missing values. larger value (e.g. 1, TRUE, second sorted level) treated treatment group; comparison group. na.rm Logical; drop rows x treatment NA. Default TRUE.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: single numeric value: Hedges' g. Positive treatment group mean exceeds comparison group mean.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: correction factor \(\omega = 1 - 3 / (4N - 9)\), \(N = n_{treatment} + n_{comparison}\).

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Hedges' g standardized mean difference — hedges_g

what: Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
title: Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
text: Plots standardized effect size covariate baseline_equivalence(), reference lines Works Clearinghouse (WWC) thresholds (0.05 0.25) points coloured WWC category. Requires ggplot2 package.

title: Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
text: equivalence data frame returned baseline_equivalence(). signed Logical. FALSE (default), plot absolute effect sizes reference lines 0.05 0.25. TRUE, plot signed effect sizes symmetric reference lines line zero.

title: Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
text: ggplot object.

title: Love plot of standardized effect sizes — love_plot
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(     treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),     pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6),     female = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1)   )   love_plot(baseline_equivalence(df, "treat")) }

title: Simulated tutoring program evaluation — tutoring
text: small, simulated (real) dataset demonstrating baseline equivalence assessment quasi-experimental education evaluation. represents 400 students: 200 received tutoring program 200 comparison students , baseline covariates measured program outcome measured . treatment group mildly positively selected, covariates span three Works Clearinghouse equivalence categories.

title: Simulated tutoring program evaluation — tutoring
text: data frame 400 rows 8 variables: treat Treatment indicator: 1 = received tutoring, 0 = comparison. pretest Baseline reading score (continuous). attendance Baseline attendance rate, 0-1 (continuous). age Age years baseline (continuous). female 1 = female, 0 = (binary). frpl Eligible free reduced-price lunch: 1 = yes (binary). ell English language learner: 1 = yes (binary). posttest Reading score program (continuous outcome).

title: Simulated tutoring program evaluation — tutoring
text: Simulated package examples data-raw/tutoring.R; real student data.

what: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: Maps standardized effect sizes three Works Clearinghouse baseline-equivalence categories. Sign ignored; classification uses absolute value effect size.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: es Numeric vector standardized effect sizes (e.g. values returned hedges_g() cox_index()).

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: character vector length es: "satisfied" |es| <= 0.05 (adjustment needed), "satisfied_with_adjustment" 0.05 < |es| <= 0.25 (equivalence holds covariate adjusted impact model), "not_satisfied" |es| > 0.25. NA inputs return NA.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Classify baseline equivalence under WWC standards — wwc_classify
Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
text: Summarizes baseline_equivalence() table one-row overall assessment: many covariates fall Works Clearinghouse (WWC) category, largest absolute effect size, overall verdict.

title: Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
text: equivalence data frame returned baseline_equivalence().

title: Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
text: one-row data frame columns n_covariates, n_satisfied, n_satisfied_with_adjustment, n_not_satisfied, max_abs_effect, overall.

title: Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
text: overall verdict follows logic categories: covariate "not_satisfied", baseline equivalence established ("not_satisfied"); otherwise, covariate requires adjustment, verdict "satisfied_with_adjustment" (equivalence holds covariates adjusted impact model); otherwise "satisfied".

title: Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
text: Works Clearinghouse (2022). Procedures Handbook (Version 5.0). U.S. Department Education.

title: Overall WWC baseline-equivalence verdict — wwc_summary
code: df <- data.frame(   treat = c(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0),   pretest = c(5, 6, 7, 4, 5, 6),   female = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1) ) wwc_summary(baseline_equivalence(df, "treat")) #>   n_covariates n_satisfied n_satisfied_with_adjustment n_not_satisfied #> 1            2           0                           0               2 #>   max_abs_effect       overall #> 1      0.8401784 not_satisfied

title: baselinr 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-30 New wwc_summary(): collapse baseline_equivalence() table overall WWC verdict (satisfied / satisfied_with_adjustment / not_satisfied) plus per-category counts largest absolute effect size. New attrition(): overall differential attrition two-group design, inputs WWC attrition standard.

title: baselinr 0.4.0
text: New bundled dataset tutoring: simulated quasi-experimental tutoring evaluation (400 students) whose covariates span three WWC equivalence categories. See data-raw/tutoring.R generated. New vignette “impact-evaluation workflow” walking raw study data baseline-equivalence report, Love plot, formatted table.

title: baselinr 0.3.0
text: New love_plot(): Love plot standardized effect sizes across covariates, WWC threshold reference lines points coloured category (requires ggplot2). New gt_baseline(): render baseline equivalence table formatted gt table readable labels rounded statistics (requires gt). ggplot2 gt added Suggests; functions error gracefully package installed.

title: baselinr 0.2.0
text: New cox_index(): WWC Cox index standardized effect size binary (dichotomous) covariates. baseline_equivalence() now handles binary covariates (numeric 0/1, logical, two-level factor) via Cox index, addition continuous covariates via Hedges’ g. covariate exactly two unique values treated binary. output gains type column ("continuous" / "binary"), effect-size column renamed hedges_g estimator-agnostic effect_size (breaking change). binary covariates, mean_treatment mean_comparison report event proportions. default covariate set now includes logical factor columns, numeric ones.

title: baselinr 0.2.0
text: Formatted output reports (gt / flextable). Love plot standardized effect sizes across covariates.

title: baselinr 0.1.0
text: Initial scaffold. hedges_g(): standardized mean difference (Hedges’ g) treatment comparison group, WWC small-sample correction factor. wwc_classify(): classify standardized mean differences three WWC baseline-equivalence categories. baseline_equivalence(): build report-ready baseline equivalence table continuous covariates.
